_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Arista Networks (ANET). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
story_narrative: Arista is the networking backbone of the AI data center. Every GPU cluster requires ultra-low-latency spine-leaf ethernet — Arista's specialty. Hyperscalers (Microsoft Azure, Meta, Google) are Arista's top customers; as they build larger GPU clusters (10K → 100K+ GPUs), they need proportionally more 400G/800G ethernet switches. Arista's EOS software platform creates switching costs — once standardized across a hyperscaler's network, it's expensive to rip out. The risk is Cisco competes on enterprise, and hyperscalers could build custom switching silicon. But Arista has won data center networking so decisively that it defines the market.
story_revenue_cagr: 17% reflects AI-driven hyperscaler networking CapEx growth. 400G to 800G upgrade cycle already underway; 1.6T ethernet is next. Enterprise campus networking adds a second growth leg.
story_target_margin: 40% EBIT margin reflects operating leverage on software (EOS) subscription and services mix growing alongside hardware. Currently ~38–40% EBIT.
story_growth_years: 10 years — GPU cluster scale-out extends through multiple AI infrastructure build cycles. Each cluster doubling requires ~proportional networking spend.
story_stable_growth: 3% — mature networking market grows with enterprise IT spend and data center builds globally.
story_stable_margin: 33% stable EBIT — as hardware commoditizes more, software and service revenue sustains margin above commodity networking peers.
story_s2c_high: 2.2x — asset-light relative to chip companies. Arista designs switches (fabless for silicon), earns software margin on EOS. Moderate capital intensity for supply chain and working capital.
story_s2c_stable: 2.5x — further improvement as EOS software and support revenue grows relative to hardware.
story_beta: 1.05 — near-market beta. Arista is more predictable than pure semis due to recurring software/support revenue, but CapEx-cycle dependent.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization for EOS software platform and silicon design IP. Networking OS and switch ASIC designs have 3–5 year relevance cycles.
story_uncertainty: Medium — strong hyperscaler relationships and EOS switching costs provide visibility. Customer concentration (top 3 = ~60% of revenue) and macro CapEx sensitivity are the primary risks.
